description_long: This is a wrought iron gate set between two robust brick pillars, each topped with a concrete or stone cap. The gate features vertical iron bars with pointed finials, providing both security and a decorative touch. The brick pillars are constructed from red and brown bricks with visible mortar lines, and the caps are a contrasting gray, likely concrete. The gate is accompanied by a matching iron fence section and a short brick base, suggesting modularity for longer barriers. Weathering and moss stains are visible at the base, indicating outdoor urban use. The overall design is utilitarian yet classic, suitable for residential or park entrances.
description: A wrought iron gate with pointed finials, set between brick pillars with concrete caps, and accompanied by a matching fence section and brick base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile of a single brick pillar with its concrete cap and iron bar details.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full gate between two brick pillars, the iron bars with pointed tops, and a separate matching fence section with a brick base.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the thinness of the gate and pillars, with the iron bars and brick base visible in profile.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the gate, pillars, and separated fence section with clear details of the brick and ironwork.
